title: Pouteria dussiana
taxonomy: Pouteria dussiana is classified in the following manner: it belongs to the family Sapotaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the sapote family. This family is further divided into several genera, with Pouteria being one of them. The genus Pouteria contains around 40 species of trees and shrubs, primarily found in tropical and subtropical regions. These plants are known for their showy flowers and edible fruits, and are commonly cultivated for their timber and food value. The classification of Pouteria dussiana is further refined by its specific species classification within the Pouteria genus.
uses: Pouteria dussiana, a lesser-known plant species, has been observed to have potential medicinal applications. Its bark, leaves, and seeds are traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy of these uses. In some parts of the world, Pouteria dussiana is used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues. It is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, although this has not been extensively studied.

parts: Pouteria dussiana is a species that has been utilized in traditional medicine. side_effects: Pouteria dussiana seeds and fruits contain a compound called amygdalin, which can release cyanide, a toxic substance, when ingested. Consuming any part of the plant can be hazardous to human health. Ingesting large amounts of the compound can cause cyanide poisoning, leading to symptoms like headaches, dizziness, and nausea. If left untreated, cyanide poisoning can be fatal. Therefore, it is essential to handle Pouteria dussiana with caution and avoid ingesting any part of the plant.
